title: Gmail Integration â€” Critical Bug Fixes
description: Fix critical bugs discovered in the new Gmail integration: token refresh regression, stage ordering, JSON parse failures, loading flash, and missing rate limiting.

userStories.title: Preserve existing refresh_token when re-connecting Gmail
userStories.description: In `backend/email_integration/service.py` `store_gmail_tokens` (lines 166-195), when `token_data.get('refresh_token')` is empty (Google omits it on re-auth when the previous token is still valid), the current code sets `gmail_refresh_token` to `''`, destroying the working token. Fix: only include `gmail_refresh_token` in the `$set` dict when `token_data.get('refresh_token')` is non-empty.
acceptanceCriteria: In store_gmail_tokens, build the $set dict conditionally: only add `gmail_refresh_token` key when `token_data.get('refresh_token')` is truthy
All other fields (access_token, expiry, email, etc.) are still updated unconditionally
No new imports required
userStories.notes: Built $set dict as a local `fields` variable and conditionally inserted gmail_refresh_token only when token_data.get('refresh_token') is truthy.

userStories.title: Set current_stage when creating application from email
userStories.description: In `backend/email_integration/service.py` `_process_message` (around line 362), `ApplicationCreate` is called with only `company`, `role_title`, and `source='email'`. If the email indicates an Interview or Assessment, the new app is incorrectly created at the default 'Applied' stage. Fix: pass `current_stage=stage` to ApplicationCreate when stage is not 'Applied'. Check that ApplicationCreate in `backend/applications/schemas.py` accepts `current_stage` as an optional field.
acceptanceCriteria: When stage != 'Applied', ApplicationCreate receives current_stage=stage
When stage == 'Applied', no change in behavior
If ApplicationCreate does not have current_stage field, add it as optional with default None
The STAGE_MAP already maps classifier output to valid stage strings
userStories.notes: Added current_stage: str | None = Field(None, ...) to ApplicationCreate in schemas.py. Updated _build_application_doc in applications/service.py to use body_dict.get('current_stage') or INITIAL_STAGE. In _process_message, pass current_stage=stage when stage != 'Applied'.

userStories.title: Stage ordering guard â€” prevent application regression
userStories.description: In `backend/email_integration/service.py` `_process_message` (around line 350), `update_application` is called with the new stage unconditionally. A stale confirmation email could downgrade an app from 'Offer' back to 'Applied'. Add a STAGE_ORDER dict at module scope mapping stage names to integers, and only call update_application if the new stage is >= the existing stage. Add STAGE_ORDER = {'Applied': 1, 'OA': 2, 'Phone Screen': 3, 'Interviewing': 4, 'Offer': 5, 'Rejected': 6} as a module-level constant.
acceptanceCriteria: STAGE_ORDER dict defined at module scope in service.py with 6 entries
In _process_message, fetch existing['current_stage'] and compare orders before updating
If new stage order <= existing stage order, skip the update and return (False, False)
If existing stage has no entry in STAGE_ORDER, allow update (defensive default)

userStories.title: Strip markdown code fences from Gemini JSON response
userStories.description: In `backend/email_integration/classifier.py` (line 68), `json.loads(content.strip())` will throw JSONDecodeError if Gemini wraps the response in ```json ... ``` fences despite the system prompt. The error is silently caught and returns None. Fix: before calling json.loads, strip markdown fences. Add a helper: `raw = re.sub(r'^```(?:json)?\s*|\s*```$', '', content.strip(), flags=re.DOTALL)` and parse `raw`. Import `re` at the top of the file.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `import re` at top of classifier.py
Before json.loads, apply regex to strip leading ```json or ``` and trailing ``` from content
json.loads parses the stripped string, not the original content
JSONDecodeError handling unchanged

userStories.title: Prevent InboxSetupBanner flash during loading
userStories.description: In `frontend/src/components/InboxSetupBanner.jsx` (line 15-19), `useGmailStatus` returns `{ data: status, isLoading }`. When `isLoading` is true, `status` is undefined, so `status?.connected` is falsy and the banner renders briefly before hiding. Fix: also destructure `isLoading` from `useGmailStatus()` and add `if (isLoading || status?.connected || dismissed) return null;`
acceptanceCriteria: Destructure isLoading from useGmailStatus()
Return null when isLoading is true
No visible banner flash on dashboard load when Gmail is already connected

userStories.title: Clear gmail_last_sync_at in _disable_gmail
userStories.description: In `backend/email_integration/batch_sync.py` `_disable_gmail` (line 73), the `$unset` dict clears credential fields but misses `gmail_last_sync_at`, `gmail_emails_scanned`, `gmail_apps_tracked`, and `gmail_status_updates_count`. This leaves stale stats in the user doc after credentials are cleared. Fix: add those four fields to the $unset dict.
acceptanceCriteria: The $unset dict in _disable_gmail includes: gmail_last_sync_at, gmail_emails_scanned, gmail_apps_tracked, gmail_status_updates_count
No other changes to the function

userStories.title: Rate-limit manual Gmail sync endpoint
userStories.description: In `backend/email_integration/router.py` (line 74), the `POST /api/email/sync` endpoint has no rate limit. Users could spam it, burning Gmail API quota. Add `@limiter.limit('3/15minutes')` decorator. Check how other rate-limited endpoints in the codebase apply `limiter` â€” look at `backend/applications/router.py` or `backend/auth/router.py` for the pattern (likely `from slowapi import Limiter` imported via `from main import limiter`). Apply the same pattern. The decorator needs a `request: Request` parameter added to `trigger_sync`.
acceptanceCriteria: trigger_sync has @limiter.limit('3/15minutes') decorator
trigger_sync signature includes request: Request parameter
Import Request from fastapi if not already imported
Pattern matches existing rate-limited endpoints in the codebase

userStories.title: Gate useGmailStatus query on authenticated state
userStories.description: In `frontend/src/hooks/useGmailStatus.js` (line 16), `useQuery` runs unconditionally, including for unauthenticated users. This fires a 401 request that triggers the token refresh interceptor unnecessarily. Fix: import `useAuth` from `../hooks/useAuth` and add `enabled: !!user` to the useQuery config. Look at `useAuth` to see how to get user (likely returns `{ user }`).
acceptanceCriteria: Import useAuth from '../hooks/useAuth' (check exact export name first)
In useGmailStatus, call useAuth() to get user
Add enabled: !!user to useQuery options
Other hooks in useGmailStatus.js (mutations) are unaffected
